notes: alright so just got on site its about 6:45, still pretty dark out. weather looks ok for now but theres meant to be rain this arvo
ok so the concreters are already here setting up for the slab pour in zone B. think theres about 6 of them plus the pump truck just rolled in
sparky's not here yet, was supposed to be here at 6:30 to finish the conduit runs before the pour. gonna give him a call
talked to Davo he said the electricians are running 20 mins late, got stuck on the freeway. should be here by 7
just checked the formwork in zone B, looks good, the carpenters did a solid job yesterday. bracing is all tight
oh wait actually theres a bit of a bow in the eastern form near grid line 7. gonna get Tommo to fix that before we pour
Tommo's on it, reckon 15-20 mins to straighten it out. no biggie
the reo in zone B has been signed off by the engineer yesterday so were good to go there once the form is sorted
site temp is about 12 degrees, overcast. wind is pretty calm maybe 5-10 kph from the west
crane operator Johnno is doing his pre-start checks now. we need the crane for lifting the precast panels on level 2 this morning
delivery truck just showed up with the precast panels. 8 panels total, theyre for the north wall level 2
hang on thats not right, its 8 panels for the north AND east walls. 5 north 3 east
sparkies finally here, 4 of them. getting straight into the conduit runs in zone A ground floor
concrete pump is set up and ready. Tommo fixed the formwork. starting the pour now, about 8:15
pour going well, nice and smooth. the mix looks good, 40 MPA as speced
wait no its 32 MPA for the slab, 40 was for the columns last week. yeah 32 is right
plumber just came up to me saying he needs access to zone C for the rough-in but theres materials stacked everywhere. need to get a laborer to clear that out
got Bazza on clearing zone C. should be done by smoko
first precast panel going up now on level 2 north wall. crane working well. Johnno's got it
safety walk with the site manager at 9. he wants to check the edge protection on level 2 before we do the precast lift
edge protection all good. harnesses are being worn by everyone up top. safety nets in place on the south side
the concrete guys are about halfway through the pour now. going to need another truck, the first ones nearly empty
second concrete truck is 15 mins out apparently
ugh just started spitting rain. not heavy but if it picks up we might have to cover the pour
got the tarps ready just in case. the concreters arent worried yet, say its fine for now
smoko break. everyone off site for 15. back at 10:15
ok back from smoko. rain stopped, looking better. suns trying to come out
second concrete truck arrived while we were on break. pour is resuming
3rd precast panel is up now. going good, no issues with the lifts
oh actually the crane had a minor hydraulic leak earlier this morning, Johnno topped up the fluid and its been fine since. should probably log that
the building inspector just showed up unannounced. wants to check the reo and formwork in zone B. told him we already got engineer sign off but hes doing his thing
inspector is happy with everything. no issues. signed off on the pour which is good
lunch time. pour in zone B is about 80% done. should finish by 1:30-2ish
alright back from lunch. pour is nearly done, maybe another 20 minutes
pour is complete. finishing crew is on it, doing the trowel work. looks really good actually
all 5 north wall panels are up now. starting on the east wall panels after lunch I mean after this, its already past lunch lol
got a delivery of timber for the level 2 formwork. 45 lengths of LVL, all 90 by 45. checked them off against the order, all good
actually its 90 by 35 not 90 by 45. my bad. the 45s are coming tomorrow
plumber Bazza finished clearing zone C and Richo's crew is in there now doing the rough-in. 3 plumbers in zone C
the apprentice Dylan dropped a hammer off level 2. hit the exclusion zone barricade below. no one was near it thank god. gave him a talking to about tool lanyards
filed an incident report for the dropped hammer. near miss. Dylan's putting a lanyard on everything now
east wall panels going up now. first one is in, nice and plumb
its getting pretty windy now, gusts maybe 30-35 kph. gonna monitor it, if it hits 40 we'll have to stop the crane lifts
wind has backed off a bit. continuing with the panels
all 3 east wall panels are done. precast is finished for the day. crane is shutting down
the concreters are still finishing the slab surface in zone B. they'll be here till about 3:30 they reckon
sparkies finished the conduit runs in zone A. theyre moving to start the cable pulling tomorrow

afternoon site walkthrough. everything looking pretty good. main things to note are the zone B slab needs to cure, no traffic on it for 24 hours minimum, and zone C plumbing rough-in is about 60% done
Richo says plumbing will be done by midday tomorrow in zone C
end of day. weather held up, no real rain in the end. had about 22 workers on site today total. good productive day. need to order more 12mm reo for next weeks column pours, running low
oh and I need to book the concrete pump again for Thursday for the zone C slab. gonna call them tonight
